usage: The standard Islamic greeting. Use it everywhere.
Casual warm hi. What friends say to each other.
Simple hello. Works in any setting.
Formal welcome. Used when receiving guests.
May God welcome you. Warm when someone enters.
Good morning.
May your morning be happy. Warmer than sabah al-khair.
Good evening.
Good night. Literally wake up to goodness.
You have lit up the place. Said when someone arrives.
How are you? Most common version.
How are you? Shorter and more casual.
What is new? Use with close friends.
How are you? Literally what is your colour. Very Saudi.
How are things? Slightly more formal.
Thank you. Universal.
Thanks said to a male. More Saudi than shukran.
May God grant you wellness. Said after someone helps you.
Bless you. Said with genuine appreciation.
May God protect you. Warm sign-off.
It is okay. No worries. One of the most used words in Gulf Arabic.
Sorry (male). Sincere apology.
Excuse me. Used to get someone's attention politely.
See you next time. Your sign-off.
Goodbye. Literally go with peace.
